description: Ralph Visual Dashboard - Web-based kanban dashboard for the Ralph autonomous AI agent loop with real-time monitoring, story management, interactive terminal, and archive browsing

userStories.title: Scaffold Next.js project with shadcn/ui and sidebar layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Next.js project scaffolded with shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS, and the sidebar layout so that all subsequent features have a foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize Next.js 14+ App Router project in `dashboard/` directory
Configure Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui with dark theme as default
Create root layout with fixed left sidebar (240px) + right main content area
Sidebar top: Ralph text logo + project name placeholder area
Flat navigation menu items with lucide-react icons: Dashboard, Stories, Terminal, Logs, Archives
Separator line, then Settings at bottom
Sidebar bottom: Ralph status indicator component (static placeholder, idle state with gray dot)
Responsive layout: sidebar collapses to hamburger menu on mobile
Each nav item links to a placeholder page at `app/(dashboard)/*/page.tsx`
npm run build succeeds with zero TypeScript errors
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use shadcn/ui Sidebar component if available, otherwise build with standard flex layout. Sidebar background zinc-950, selected item zinc-800.

userStories.title: Add TypeScript types and PRD read API endpoints
userStories.description: As the dashboard frontend, I need API endpoints to read prd.json so that I can display task status.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared TypeScript types in `lib/types.ts`: PRD (project, branchName, description, userStories) and Story (id, title, description, acceptanceCriteria, priority, passes, notes)
Create config helper in `lib/config.ts` that reads `dashboard/config.json` with defaults (prd path: `../scripts/ralph/prd.json`)
GET /api/prd returns full prd.json content
GET /api/prd/stories returns userStories array
GET /api/prd/stories/[id] returns single story by id
File not found returns 404 with `{ data: null, error: 'PRD file not found' }`
All API responses use format `{ data: T | null, error: string | null }`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use path.resolve for cross-platform path handling. Config file is optional - use defaults when missing.

userStories.title: Add PRD story CRUD API endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create, edit, and delete user stories through the dashboard so that I don't need to manually edit JSON files.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/prd/stories creates new story with auto-incremented ID (US-XXX format based on max existing ID + 1)
PUT /api/prd/stories/[id] updates story fields: title, description, acceptanceCriteria, priority, passes, notes
DELETE /api/prd/stories/[id] deletes specified story
PATCH /api/prd updates project-level fields (project, branchName, description)
PATCH /api/prd/stories/reorder accepts array of {id, priority} to batch-update priority order
All write operations use atomic file write (write to .tmp then rename) to prevent corruption
Input validation: title required on create, returns 400 with `{ data: null, error: 'title is required' }`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use fs.writeFile to temp file then fs.rename for atomic writes. Lock file writes with a simple in-memory mutex to prevent race conditions.

userStories.title: Add progress log and git history API endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view the progress log so that I can see what Ralph has learned and done.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/logs/progress returns progress.txt parsed into structured array by `---` separator
Each parsed record contains: date, storyId, summary, filesChanged (string[]), learnings (string[])
GET /api/logs/git returns last 50 git commits via simple-git (hash, message, date, author)
File not found returns `{ data: [], error: null }` (empty array, not error)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Install simple-git as dependency. Parse progress.txt with regex for ## headers and bullet points.

userStories.title: Add Ralph process control API endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want to start and stop Ralph from the dashboard so that I don't need to switch to the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Create process manager singleton in `lib/ralph-process.ts` using child_process.spawn
POST /api/ralph/start spawns ralph.sh with params from body: `{ tool: 'claude' | 'amp', maxIterations: number }`
POST /api/ralph/stop terminates running Ralph process (Windows: taskkill /pid /t /f, Unix: SIGTERM)
POST /api/ralph/stdin sends input string to Ralph process stdin: `{ input: string }`
GET /api/ralph/status returns `{ status: 'idle'|'running'|'completed'|'error', iteration: number, startedAt: string|null }`
Duplicate start returns 409 `{ data: null, error: 'Ralph is already running' }`
Process exit automatically cleans up reference and updates status to idle or error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Store process reference and status in module-level singleton. Detect iteration number by parsing stdout for 'Ralph Iteration X of Y' pattern.

userStories.title: Add WebSocket real-time communication server
userStories.description: As a user, I want the dashboard to update in real-time so that I can see Ralph's progress without refreshing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create standalone WebSocket server on port 3001 using `ws` library in `server/ws.ts`
Define event types: ralph:output, ralph:status, ralph:iteration, prd:updated, progress:updated
Client can send ralph:stdin event to forward input to Ralph process stdin
Use chokidar to watch prd.json and progress.txt, debounce 500ms, broadcast prd:updated/progress:updated on change
Ralph process stdout/stderr pipes to WebSocket as ralph:output events (preserving ANSI sequences)
All messages use format: `{ type: string, payload: object, timestamp: string }`
Add `concurrently` to package.json: `npm run dev` starts both Next.js dev server and WebSocket server
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: WebSocket server imports the ralph-process singleton from US-005 to pipe stdout. Install chokidar and concurrently as dependencies.

userStories.title: Add WebSocket hooks and global state management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared WebSocket hooks and a global state store so that all pages can access real-time data consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `useWebSocket` hook in `hooks/use-websocket.ts`: manages connection to ws://localhost:3001, auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, max 30s), event subscribe/unsubscribe
Create `useRalphStatus` hook: returns Ralph status (idle/running/completed/error), current iteration number, elapsed time
Create `usePrd` hook: returns PRD data, auto-refreshes on prd:updated WebSocket event
Use Zustand store in `lib/store.ts` for global state: Ralph status + PRD data + WebSocket connection state
Sidebar status indicator component reads from Zustand store and displays correct color/animation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Install zustand. Hooks should be client components ('use client'). WebSocket connection is established once at app level and shared via store.

userStories.title: Build Dashboard kanban overview page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a kanban-style dashboard showing task completion status at a glance with three columns.
acceptanceCriteria: Page top info bar: project name, branch name, project description, Ralph status indicator (linked to global state)
Stats card row: total tasks, completed count, pending count, completion percentage with circular progress ring
Three-column kanban layout: Pending (passes=false, not current) / In Progress (story Ralph is currently working on) / Completed (passes=true)
Kanban cards show: ID badge, title text, priority color left-border (high=red-500, medium=amber-500, low=zinc-500), passes status icon
Click card opens shadcn/ui Dialog with full story details and edit form
Drag-and-drop cards between columns using @hello-pangea/dnd: dropping to Completed sets passes=true, dropping out sets passes=false
In Progress column card shows loading animation when Ralph is running
Data auto-refreshes via WebSocket prd:updated events
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Install @hello-pangea/dnd. In Progress detection: compare current iteration story ID from Ralph status. Columns are equal width with 8px card gap.

userStories.title: Build user story management page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dedicated page to manage all user stories with full CRUD operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Page header: title 'User Stories' + 'New Story' button
Card list displays all stories, each card shows: ID badge, title, priority tag (color-coded), passes toggle switch
New Story button opens shadcn/ui Sheet (slide-over panel) with form: title (required), description (textarea), acceptanceCriteria (dynamic add/remove rows), priority (Select: high/medium/low)
Click card opens Sheet in edit mode with all fields populated and editable
Delete button triggers shadcn/ui AlertDialog confirmation before deletion
Toggle passes directly on card without opening details
Drag-to-reorder cards to adjust priority (auto-calls reorder API on drop)
All operations write to prd.json immediately and sync via WebSocket
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Reuse @hello-pangea/dnd from US-008 for drag sorting. Sheet component from shadcn/ui for side panel.

userStories.title: Build interactive terminal page with xterm.js and real-time streaming
userStories.description: As a user, I want a terminal page to see Ralph's real-time output and send input to the Ralph process.
acceptanceCriteria: Install xterm, xterm-addon-fit, xterm-addon-web-links as dependencies
Create Terminal component using next/dynamic with ssr:false to avoid SSR errors
Terminal styling: dark background (#1a1a2e), JetBrains Mono font, full ANSI color/bold/underline rendering
Real-time mode: when Ralph is running, stream stdout/stderr via WebSocket ralph:output events into xterm
Input capability: keyboard input in terminal sends text via WebSocket ralph:stdin to Ralph process stdin
Page top bar: Ralph status badge, current iteration number, elapsed time timer
Quick start/stop buttons at top (calls /api/ralph/start and /api/ralph/stop)
When Ralph is not running, terminal shows welcome message with start instructions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: xterm.js must be dynamically imported to avoid SSR issues. Use xterm-addon-fit for responsive terminal sizing.

userStories.title: Add terminal history mode with iteration tabs and toolbar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to review historical iteration outputs and have toolbar controls for the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab bar above terminal: 'Live' tab + per-iteration tabs ('Iteration 1', 'Iteration 2', etc.)
Iteration tabs auto-created when ralph:iteration events are received, output stored in frontend memory per iteration
Clicking iteration tab loads that iteration's buffered output into a read-only xterm instance
Live tab shows current real-time stream (default when Ralph is running)
Toolbar buttons: Clear output, Pause/Resume auto-scroll toggle, Download current output as .log file
After Ralph completes, show summary: stories completed count, total elapsed time
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Detect iteration boundaries by parsing 'Ralph Iteration X of Y' pattern in output stream. Store iteration buffers in Zustand or component state.

userStories.title: Build iteration logs page with progress timeline and git history
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view Ralph's iteration logs and git history so that I can understand what was done and learned.
acceptanceCriteria: Top tab navigation using shadcn/ui Tabs: Progress Log / Git History
Progress Log tab: timeline layout, each entry shows date, Story ID (clickable link to /stories), implementation summary, changed files (code-styled tags)
Learnings section highlighted with distinct background (dark purple card)
Codebase Patterns section pinned at top if present in progress.txt
Git History tab: list of commits showing short hash (monospace), message, date, author
Ralph commits (starting with 'feat:') marked with special badge
Search input filters log entries and commits by keyword
Real-time: new entries prepend to top with fade-in animation when progress:updated event received
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use shadcn/ui Badge for tags and status indicators. Timeline can use a simple vertical line + dot pattern with Tailwind.

userStories.title: Build archive browsing page with read-only views
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse archived Ralph runs so that I can review previous feature development history.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/archives scans scripts/ralph/archive/ directory, returns list of archive folders (date + feature name)
GET /api/archives/[folder] returns archived prd.json and progress.txt contents
Page displays archive list: each item shows date, branch/feature name, story completion stats (e.g. '3/4 completed')
Click archive item expands details: read-only kanban view (reuse kanban components from US-008) + read-only log view
Empty state shown when no archives exist
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Reuse KanbanBoard component from US-008 with a readOnly prop that disables drag-and-drop and edit actions.

userStories.title: Build settings page with configuration form
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure dashboard and Ralph settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings form built with shadcn/ui Form components
Path Configuration group: Ralph script path (default ../scripts/ralph/ralph.sh), PRD file path (default ../scripts/ralph/prd.json), Progress file path (default ../scripts/ralph/progress.txt)
Default Parameters group: default AI tool select (Claude/Amp dropdown), default max iterations (number input, default 10)
Interface group: terminal font size slider (12-24px)
Save button writes config to dashboard/config.json via POST /api/config
Reset to Defaults button restores all fields to default values
Page loads config from GET /api/config on mount, uses defaults when config.json missing
Save success shows shadcn/ui Toast notification
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Add GET /api/config and POST /api/config endpoints. Config file is dashboard/config.json.
